components.name: BrandOneShowcaseAppComponent
components.templateData:
Angular app · external CSS brand pack

Angular consumes only wrapper logic, then Brand 1 arrives from CSS.

This application imports the Angular wrapper plus the Brand 1 light and dark packs. The component library itself stays visually neutral until the external stylesheet is present.

Theme pack

@for (themeOption of themeOptions; track themeOption.value) { }
Brand 1 default Class-based override Inline token tweak

All variants

Component API

Default Default button
Disabled Disabled state
Full width Full-width layout
Submit Type: submit
Reset Type: reset
Pill shape Class override (border-radius: 999px)
Custom tokens Inline token override
→ Next Slotted icon text

Consumer imports

Import the Angular wrapper package.
Import the shared token contract.
Import the Brand 1 light stylesheet.
Import the Brand 1 dark stylesheet.
Set data-brand="brand-1" and the active theme on the outer shell.
Render the same ff-button logic inside that shell.

Theme behavior

The shell flips between light and dark.
The button keeps the same logic API in both modes.
Brand 1 colors come entirely from the imported CSS pack.
The current PoC deliberately ships only the primitive represented in Figma: the button.

What proves the separation

The Angular wrapper provides framework integration only.
The imported CSS library gives the primitives their brand and surface tokens.
The same wrapper logic swaps between light and dark with CSS alone.
Host classes and per-instance CSS properties still tailor the component locally.
Removing a non-Figma primitive leaves the Angular bridge untouched for the shipped button.
